_metadata.title: Classification of General-Purpose AI Models with Systemic Risk

primary_official_source.name: EUR-Lex -- Official Journal of the European Union

upcoming: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for GPAI classification (expected 2027)

obligations.source_quote: A general-purpose AI model shall be classified as a general-purpose AI model with systemic risk if it meets any of the following conditions: (a) it has high impact capabilities evaluated on the basis of appropriate technical tools and methodologies, including indicators and benchmarks; (b) based on a decision of the Commission, ex officio or following a qualified alert from the scientific panel, it has capabilities or an impact equivalent to those set out in point (a) having regard to the criteria set out in Annex XIII.

decomposed_atoms.description: Classification as systemic risk GPAI based on high impact capabilities or Commission decision
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL be classified as systemic risk if high impact capabilities OR Commission designation

obligations.source_quote: A general-purpose AI model shall be presumed to have high impact capabilities pursuant to paragraph 1, point (a), when the cumulative amount of computation used for its training measured in floating point operations is greater than 10^25.

decomposed_atoms.description: Models trained with > 10^25 FLOPs are presumed systemic risk
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL be presumed high impact when training compute > 10^25 FLOPs

obligations.source_quote: The Commission shall adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 97 to amend the thresholds listed in paragraphs 1 and 2 of this Article, as well as to supplement benchmarks and indicators in light of evolving technological developments, such as algorithmic improvements or increased hardware efficiency, when necessary, for these thresholds to reflect the state of the art.

decomposed_atoms.description: Commission empowered to update the 10^25 FLOPs threshold via delegated acts
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Commission SHALL adopt delegated acts to amend thresholds as technology evolves
